method_atom: A distributed, asynchronous algorithm in which many autonomous agents, communicating only through sporadic, short-range, unreliable pairwise exchanges, iteratively reassign ownership of the elements of a discrete set (the vertices of a graph) using a purely local pairwise update rule. Cast as a dynamical system on the space of partitions of that set, the process is proven to converge in finite time to a pairwise-optimal equilibrium partition, and it runs anytime with bounded per-agent resources and scales to large numbers of agents.
object_atom: A team of mobile robots deployed to partition and provide coverage of a non-convex physical environment, where the environment is discretized as a graph and neighboring robots exchange only intermittent short-range "gossip" messages.
brief_reason: The transferable machinery is the local pairwise-partition-reassignment dynamics over a discrete set that provably converges to a pairwise-optimal equilibrium under unreliable gossip communication, while the mobile-robots-covering-a-non-convex-environment system is the robotics-specific object.
